author: John Chrysostom
book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.

classification.cautionReason: No major mainstream heresy warning is attached by the preview classifier.

lineage: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.

ccel.title: NPNF1-10. St. Chrysostom: Homilies on the Gospel of Saint Matthew

source.title: NPNF1-10. St. Chrysostom: Homilies on the Gospel of Saint Matthew

title: Matthew XII. 38, 39.

source.title: NPNF1-10. St. Chrysostom: Homilies on the Gospel of Saint Matthew

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “Then certain of the Scribes and Pharisees answered Him, saying, Master, we would see a sign from Thee.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But He answered and said,An evil and adulterous generation seeketh after a sign, and there shall no sign be given to it, but the sign of the Prophet Jonas.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Could then anything be more foolish than these men (not more impious only), who after so many miracles, as though none had been wrought, say, “We would see a sign from Thee?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: With what intent then did they so speak?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: That they might lay hold of Him again.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For since by His words He had stopped their mouths, once and twice and often, and had checked their shameless tongue, they come to His works again.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: At which also the evangelist marvelling again, said,

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “Then certain of the scribes answered Him, asking a sign.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “Then,” when?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: When they ought to be stooping before Him, to admire, to be amazed and give way, “then” they desist not from their wickedness.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And see their words too, teeming with flattery and dissimulation.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For they thought to draw Him towards them in that way.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And now they insult, now they flatter Him; now calling Him a demoniac, now again “Master,” both out of an evil mind, how contrary soever the words they speak.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Wherefore also He rebukes them severely.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And when they were questioning Him roughly and insulting Him, He reasoned with them gently; when they were flattering; reproachfully, and with great severity; implying that He is superior to either passion, and is neither at the one time moved to anger, nor at the other softened by flattery.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And see His reproach, that it is not merely hard words, but contains a demonstration of their wickedness.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For what saith He?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “An evil and adulterous generation seeketh after a sign.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Now what He saith is to this effect: What marvel if ye behave so to me who have been hitherto unknown to you when even to the Father, of whom ye have had so much experience, ye have done the very same? forsaking Him, ye have run unto the devils, drawing to yourselves wicked lovers.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: With this Ezekiel too was continually upbraiding them.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Now by these sayings He signified Himself to be of one accord with His Father, and them to be doing nothing new; He was also unfolding their secrets, how with hypocrisy and as enemies they were making their demand.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Therefore He called them “an evil generation,” because they have been always ungrateful towards their benefactors; because upon favors they become worse, which belongs to extreme wickedness.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And He called it “adulterous,” declaring both their former and their present unbelief; whereby He implies Himself again to be equal to the Father, if at least the not believing Him makes it “adulterous.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Then, after His reproach, what saith He?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “There shall no sign be given to it, but the sign of Jonas the prophet.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Now is He striking the first note of the doctrine of His resurrection, and confirming it by the type.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: What then? one may say; was no sign given it?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: None was given to it on asking.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For not to bring in them did He work His signs (for He knew them to be hardened), but in order to amend others.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Either then this may be said, or that they were not to receive such a sign as that was.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For a sign did befall them, when by their own punishment they learnt His power.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Here then He speaks as threatening, and with this very meaning obscurely conveyed: as if He said, innumerable benefits have I showed forth, none of these hath drawn you to me, neither were ye willing to adore my power.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Ye shall know therefore my might by the contrary tokens, when ye shall see your city cast down to the ground, the walls also dismantled, the temple become a ruin; when ye shall be cast out both from your former citizenship and freedom, and shall again go about everywhere, houseless and in exile.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: (For all these things came to pass after the cross.)

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: These things therefore shall be to you for great signs.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And indeed it is an exceeding great sign, that their ills remain unchanged; that although ten thousand have attempted it, no one hath been able to reversethe judgment once gone forth against them.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: All this however He saith not, but leaves it to after time to make it clear to them, but for the present He is making trial ofthe doctrine of His resurrection, which they were to come to know by the things which they should afterwards suffer.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “For as Jonas,” saith He, “was three days and three nights in the whale’s belly, so shall the Son of Man be three days and three nights in the heart of the earth.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Thus, He said not indeed openly that He should rise again, since they would have even laughed Him to scorn, but He intimated it in such manner, that they might believe Him to have foreknown it.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For as to their being aware of it, they say to Pilate, “That deceiver said,” these are their words, “while He was yet alive, After three days I will rise again;”and yet we know His disciples were ignorant of this; even as they had been beforehand more void of understanding than these: wherefore also these became self-condemned.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But see how exactly He expresses it, even though in a dark saying.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For He said not, “In the earth,” but, “In the heart of the earth;” that He might designate His very sepulchre, and that no one might suspect a mere semblance.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And for this intent too did He allow three days, that the fact of His death might be believed.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For not by the cross only doth He make it certain, and by the sight of all men, but also by the time of those days.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For to the resurrection indeed all succeeding time was to bear witness; but the cross, unless it had at the time many signs bearing witness to it, would have been disbelieved; and with this disbelief would have gone utter disbelief of the resurrection also.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Therefore He calls it also a sign.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But had He not been crucified, the sign would not have been given.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For this cause too He brings forward the type, that the truth may be believed.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For tell me, was Jonah in the whale’s belly a mere appearance?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Nay, thou canst not say so.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Therefore neither was Christ in the heart of the earth such.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For surely the type is not in truth, and the truth in mere appearance.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For this cause we every where show forth His death, both in the mysteries, and in baptism, and in all the rest.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Therefore Paul also cries with a clear voice, “God forbid that I should glory, save in the cross of our Lord Jesus Christ.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Whence it is clear, that they who are diseased in Marcion’s way are children of the devil, blotting out these truths, to avoid the annulling whereof Christ did so many things, while to have them annulled the devil took such manifold pains: I mean, His cross and His passion.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Therefore He said elsewhere also, “Destroy this temple, and in three days I will raise it up:”and, “The days will come when the Bridegroom shall be taken away from them:and here, “There shall no sign be given it, but the sign of Jonas the prophet:” declaring both that He should dieand “the ox that knoweth his owner, and the ass that remembereth his crib.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Even so here too, when He had by a comparison set forth their perverseness, He speaks afterwards of their punishment also.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: What then can the saying mean?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: As the possessed, saith He, when delivered from that infirmity, should they be at all remiss, draw upon themselves their delusion more grievous than ever: even so is it with you.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For before also ye were possessed by a devil, when ye were worshipping idols, and were slaying your sons to the devils, exhibiting great madness; nevertheless I forsook you not, but cast out that devil by the prophets; and again in my own person I am come, willing to cleanse you more entirely.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Since then you will not attend, but have wrecked yourselves in greater wickedness (for to kill prophets was a crime not nearly so great and grievous as to slay Him); therefore your sufferings will be more grievous than the former, those at Babylon, I mean, and in Egypt, and under the first Antiochus.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Because what things befell them in the time of Vespasian and Titus, were very far more grievous than those.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Wherefore also He said, “There shall be great tribulation, such as never was, neither shall be.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But not this only doth the illustration declare, but that they should be also utterly destitute of all virtue, and more assailable by the power of the devils, than at that time.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For then even although they sinned, yet were there also among them such as acted uprightly, and God’s providence was present with them, and the grace of the Spirit, tending, correcting, fulfilling all its part; but now of this guardianship too they shall be utterly deprived; so He tells them; so that there is now both a greater scarcity of virtue, and a more intense affliction, and a more tyrannical operation of the devils.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Ye know accordingly even in our generation, when he who surpassed all in impiety, I mean Julian, was transported with his fury, how they ranged themselves with the heathens, how they courted their party.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: So that, even if they seem to be in some small degree chastened now, the fear of the emperors makes them quiet; since, if it were not for that, far worse than the former had been their daring.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For in all their other evil works they surpass their predecessors; sorceries, magic arts, impurities, they exhibit in great excess.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And amongst the rest, moreover, strong as is the curb which holds them down, they have often made seditions, and risen up against kings, which has resulted in their being pierced through with the worst of evils.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Where now are they that seek after signs?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Let them hear that a considerate mind is needed, and if this be wanting, signs are of no profit.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: See, for instance, how the Ninevites without signs believed, while these, after so many miracles, grew worse, and made themselves an habitation of innumerable devils, and brought on themselves ten thousand calamities; and very naturally.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For when a man, being once delivered from his ills, fails to be corrected, he will suffer far worse than before.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Yea, therefore He said, “he finds no rest,” to indicate, that positively and of necessity such an one will be overtaken by the ambush of the devils.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Since surely by these two things he ought to have been sobered, by his former sufferings, and by his deliverance; or rather a third thing also is added, the threat of having still worse to endure.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But yet by none of these were they made better.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: All this might be seasonably said, not of them only, but of us also, when after having been enlightened,and delivered from our former ills, we again cleave unto the same wickedness, for more grievous also thenceforth will be the punishment of our subsequent sins.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Therefore to the sick of the palsy also Christ said, “Behold, thou art made whole; sin no more, lest a worse thing come unto thee;”and this to a man who was thirty-eight years in his infirmity.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And what, one might ask, was he to suffer worse than this?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Something far worse, and more intolerable.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For far be it from us, that we should endure as much as we are capable of enduring.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For God is at no loss for inflictions.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For according to the greatness of His mercy, so also is His wrath.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: With this He charges Jerusalem also by Ezekiel.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “I saw thee,” saith He, “polluted in blood; and I washed thee, and anointed thee; and thou hadst renown for thy beauty; and thou pouredst out thy fornications,” saith He, “on those who dwell near thee,”wherefore also the more grievous are His threatenings to thee when thou sinnest.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But from hence infer not thy punishment only, but also the boundless longsuffering of God.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: How often at least have we put our hands to the same evil deeds, and yet He suffers long!

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But let us not be sanguine, but fear; since Pharaoh too, had he been taught by the first plague, would not have experienced the later ones; he would not afterwards have been drowned, his host and all together.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And this I say, because I know many, who like Pharaoh are even now saying, “I know not God,”and making those that are in their power cleave to the clay and to the bricks.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: How many, though God bids them assauge their “threatening,”cannot bear so much as to relax the toil!

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “But we have no Red Sea now, to pass through afterwards.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But we have a sea of fire, a sea not like that, either in kind or in size, but far greater and fiercer, having its waves of fire, of some strange and horrible fire.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: A great abyss is there, of most intolerable flame.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Since everywhere fire may be seen roving quickly round, like some savage wild beast.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And if here this sensible and material fire leaped like a wild beast out of the furnace, and sprang upon those who were sitting without,what will not that other fire do to such as have fallen into it?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Concerning that day, hear the prophets, saying, “The day of the Lord is incurable, full of anger and wrath.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For there will be none to stand by, none to rescue, nowhere the face of Christ, so mild and calm.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But as those who work in the mines are delivered over to certain cruel men, and see none of their friends, but those only that are set over them; so will it be then also: or rather not so, but even far more grievous.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For here it is possible to go unto the king, and entreat, and free the condemned person: but there, no longer; for He permits it not, but they continue in the scorching torment,and in so great anguish, as it is not possible for words to tell.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For if, when any are in flames here, no speech can describe their sharp pangs, much less theirs, who suffer it in that place: since here indeed all is over in a brief point of time, but in that place there is burning indeed, but what is burnt is not consumed.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: What then shall we do there?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For to my self also do I say these things.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “But if thou,” saith one, “who art our teacher, speakest so of thyself, I care no more; for what wonder, should I be punished?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Nay, I entreat, let no man seek this consolation; for this is no refreshment at all.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For tell me; was not the devil an incorporeal power?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Was he not superior to men?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Yet he fell away.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Is there any one who will derive consolation from being punished along with him?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: By no means.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: What of all who were in Egypt? did they not see those also punished who were in high places, and every house in mourning?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Were they then hereby refreshed, and comforted?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: No surely; and it is manifest by what they did afterwards, as men tortured by some kind of fire, rising up together against the king, and compelling him to cast out the people of the Hebrews.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Yea, and very unmeaning is this saying, to suppose that it gives comfort to be punished with all men, to say, “As all, so I too.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For why should I speak of hell?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Think, I pray you, of those that are seized with gout, how, when they are racked by sharp pain, though you show them ten thousand suffering worse, they do not so much as take it into their mind.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For the intensity of their anguish allows not their reason any leisure for thinking of others, and so finding consolation.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Let us not then feed ourselves with these cold hopes.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For to receive consolation from the ills of our neighbors, takes place in ordinary sufferings; but when the torment is excessive, and all our inward parts full of tempest, and the soul is now come to be unable so much as to know itself, whence shall it derive consolation?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: So that all these sayings are an absurdity, and fables of foolish children.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For this, of which thou speakest, takes place in dejection, and in moderate dejection, when we are told, “the same thing hath befallen such an one;” but sometimes not even in dejection: now if in that case it hath no strength, much less in the anguish and burden unspeakable, which “the gnashing of teeth” indicates.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And I know that I am galling you, and giving you pain by these words; but what can I do?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For I would fain not speak thus, but be conscious of virtue both in myself, and in all of you; but since we are in sins, the more part of us, who will grant me ability to pain you indeed, and to penetrate the understanding of them that hear me?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Then might I so be at rest.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But now I fear lest any despise my sayings, and their punishments become the greater for their indifferent way of hearing.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Since, when a master utters a threat, should one of the fellow-servants hear and make light of his menace, not without punishment would he hasten by him, provoked as he is, but rather it would be a ground for increasing his chastisement.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Wherefore I entreat you, let us pierce our own hearts, when we hear His sayings regarding hell.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For nothing is more delightful than this discourse, by how much nothing is more bitter than the reality.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But how delightful to be told of hell? one may ask.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Because it were so far from delight to fall into hell, which result, our words that appear so galling, keep off.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And before this they furnish another pleasure: in that they brace up our souls, and make us more reverent, and elevate the mind, and give wings to the thoughts, and cast out the desires that so mischievously beset us; and the thing becomes a cure.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Wherefore, to proceed, together with the punishment let me speak also of the shame.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For as the Jews shall then be condemned by the Ninevites, so we too by many that seem beneath us now.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Let us imagine then how great the mockery, how great the condemnation; let us imagine, and cast some foundation at length, some door of repentance.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: To myself I say these things, to myself first I give this advice, and let no one be angry, as though he were condemned.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Let us enter upon the narrow way.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: How long shall it be luxury? how long sloth?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Have we not had enough of indolence, mirth, procrastination?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Will it not be the same over again, feasting, and surfeiting, and expense, and wealth, and acquisitions, and buildings?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And what is the end?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: What is the end?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Ashes, and dust, and coffins, and worms.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Let us show forth then a new kind of life.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Let us make earth, heaven; let us hereby show the Greeks, of how great blessings they are deprived.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For when they behold in us good conversation, they will look upon the very face of the kingdom of Heaven.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Yea, when they see us gentle, pure from wrath, from evil desire, from envy, from covetousness, rightly fulfilling all our other duties, they will say, “If the Christians are become angels here, what will they be after their departure hence? if where they are strangers they shine so bright, how great will they become when they shall have won their native land!”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Thus they too will be reformed, and the word of godliness “will have free course,”not less than in the apostles’ times.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For if they, being twelve, converted entire cities and countries; were we all to become teachers by our careful conduct, imagine how high our cause will be exalted.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For not even a dead man raised so powerfully attracts the Greek, as a person practising self-denial.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: At that indeed he will be amazed, but by this he will be profited.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: That is done, and is past away; but this abides, and is constant culture to his soul.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Let us take heed therefore to ourselves, that we may gain them also.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: I say nothing burdensome.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: I say not, do not marry.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: I say not, forsake cities, and withdraw thyself from public affairs; but being engaged in them, show virtue.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Yea, and such as are busy in the midst of cities, I would fain have more approved than such as have occupied the mountains.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Because great is the profit thence arising.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “For no man lighteth a candle, and setteth it under the bushel.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Therefore I would that all the candles were set upon the candlestick, that the light might wax great.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Let us kindle then His fire; let us cause them that are sitting in darkness to be delivered from their error.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And tell me not, “I have a wife, and children belonging to me, and am master of a household, and cannot duly practise all this.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For though thou hadst none of these, yet if thou be careless, all is lost; though thou art encompassed with all these, yet if thou be earnest, thou shalt attain unto virtue.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For there is but one thing that is wanted, the preparation of a generous mind; and neither age, nor poverty, nor wealth, nor reverse of fortune, nor anything else, will be able to impede thee.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Since in fact both old and young, and men having wives, and bringing up children, and working at crafts, and serving as soldiers, have duly performed all that is enjoined.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For so Daniel was young, and Joseph a slave, and Aquila wrought at a craft, and the woman who sold purple was over a workshop, and another was the keeper of a prison, and another a centurion, as Cornelius; and another in ill health, as Timothy; and another a runaway, as Onesimus; but nothing proved an hindrance to any of these, but all were approved, both men and women, both young and old, both slaves and free, both soldiers and people.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Let us not then make vain pretexts, but let us provide a thoroughly good mind, and whatsoever we may be, we shall surely attain to virtue, and arrive at the good things to come; by the grace and love towards man of our Lord Jesus Christ, with whom be unto the Father, together with the Holy Ghost, glory, might, honor, now and ever, and world without end.
